title: CI UNSTABLE Gates Identically to Red — no merge on UNSTABLE
statement: GitHub Pull Request mergeStateStatus UNSTABLE MUST gate identically to FAILING. No squash-merge proceeds while mergeStateStatus = UNSTABLE absent explicit named-step approval AND substrate proof that the failure is not introduced by the PR. Even with proof, the correct move is fix-baseline (on a separate PR or by reverting the offending main commit), not merge-anyway.
rationale: UNSTABLE collapses to 'mergeable with context' in the GitHub UI, creating an attractive nuisance: the merge button is available and the failures 'look like someone else's problem.' Two incidents prove this is a pattern not a one-off: PR #48 (2026-04-XX) silently bypassed pre-existing CI; PR #269 (2026-05-30) repeated the same failure mode under different cover. The system's correct response is fix-baseline, not merge-anyway with audit annotation.

authority.author: EB-agent (chat), L1; relayed via schema-registry chat (Claude L1)

human-loop.gate: Chat layer refuses squash-merge when mergeStateStatus is UNSTABLE without explicit named-step authorization from Erik (L4). REFUSE-AND-SURFACE is the canonical response (memory #15).
human-loop.automationPath: GitHub Actions workflow that fails on PR check when mergeStateStatus = UNSTABLE unless a specific override label is present on the PR. Override label requires explicit application by Erik; chat layer cannot apply it.

automated.predicate: A PR with mergeStateStatus = UNSTABLE cannot be squash-merged via gh pr merge unless the PR carries an explicit override label applied by Erik (L4).
automated.ciSurface: GitHub Actions workflow on PR — checks gh pr view mergeStateStatus before squash-merge.

refine.action: Branch off main, edit this rule instance, validate, PR with refinement rationale.

transitions.trigger: Rule instance authored 2026-06-01; statement captured from EB-agent contribution relaying PR #48 and PR #269 incident pattern.

transitions.evidenceClass: Two incidents establish pattern, not one-off: PR #48 (2026-04-XX) silently bypassed pre-existing CI failures; PR #269 (2026-05-30) repeated the failure mode under different cover. Rule statement documented in memory #15 prose (REFUSE-AND-SURFACE) but never schematized until this instance.
transitions.trigger: Rule promoted from working-model to active human-loop enforcement after PR #269 repeat incident confirmed pattern.

transitions.evidenceClass: CI workflow .github/workflows/pr-mergeability-check.yml implemented in d-2026-06-01-ci-unstable-merge-blocker; blocks PR merge when mergeStateStatus = UNSTABLE unless Erik (L4) applies override-unstable-merge label. Workflow self-tests on its own PR before landing. Step 2 substrate read confirmed mergeStateStatus async behavior (UNSTABLE→CLEAN transition observed ~2s apart); retry loop implemented.
transitions.trigger: Rule promoted via canonical mechanism: workflow authored in FU-D-2 dispatch, validated by self-passing on this PR; tier flips on merge.

gatingBinding.gatedWork: any PR squash-merge across all LuhTech repositories
gatingBinding.ciSurface: PENDING — GitHub Actions workflow per gateActions.automated.ciSurface

notes: Originated via EB-agent contribution (Evidence #1) relayed to schema-registry chat. Real incidents: PR #48 + PR #269 (repeat). Pairs with memory #15 REFUSE-AND-SURFACE (prose form of the same principle, now schematized). Same axis as RULE-CI-DOCUMENTED-OVERRIDE-NOT-COMPLIANCE-1 — both encode CI gating discipline. Credit: EB-agent for the rule shape; Claude (schema-registry chat) for the instance authoring. | PROMOTED 2026-06-01: tierState.activeTier human-loop → automated via FU-D-2 (d-2026-06-01-ci-unstable-merge-blocker). First rule promotion in foundation arc's post-completion FU block. Proves the maturity ladder works as designed.
